evals.prompt: Determine the cross-section parameters for a Class II Paved road in Uganda. The road is in a rural area with rolling terrain. The design speed is 70 km/h per MoWT standard. Using the MoWT Road Design Manual, specify the lane width, shoulder width, carriageway width, crossfall, and road reserve width for the rural/normal cross-section.
evals.expected_output: From MoWT Figure 7-14 (rural/normal cross-section) for Paved II: lane width 3.0m, shoulder width 2.0m, carriageway width 6.0m (2 lanes x 3.0m), roadway width 10.0m, road reserve width 30.0m, crossfall 2.5% (paved road).

assertions.text: Lane width is 3.0m for Class II Paved rural section
Shoulder width is 2.0m for Class II Paved rural section
Carriageway width is 6.0m (2 lanes x 3.0m)
Crossfall is 2.5% for paved road (not 4.0% which applies to gravel)
References MoWT Road Design Manual Figure 7-14 or Tables 4-3 to 4-9 for parameter values

evals.prompt: A UK trunk road is classified as a WS2 (wide single 2-lane) carriageway under DMRB CD 109. Determine the carriageway width and note any additional cross-section information available from CD 109. The road is a rural all-purpose road with kerbed drainage sections.
evals.expected_output: From CD 109 Table 2.3: WS2 carriageway width is 7.3m. Minimum gradient on kerbed sections is 0.5% (CD 109 clause 5.2). Full cross-section design (verge widths, headrooms, central reserve) requires CD 127 which provides detailed cross-section parameters beyond carriageway width.

assertions.text: Carriageway width is 7.3m for WS2 road type per CD 109 Table 2.3
References CD 127 as required for full cross-section detail beyond carriageway width
Notes minimum gradient of 0.5% on kerbed roads per CD 109 clause 5.2
Distinguishes between CD 109 (carriageway types) and CD 127 (full cross-section design) responsibilities

evals.prompt: A Gravel C road in Uganda passes through a small trading centre (town section). Determine the cross-section parameters for the town section. Note any concerns about a single-lane road in a town context and recommend whether an escalation flag is appropriate.
evals.expected_output: From MoWT Figure 7-16 (town gravel) for Gravel C: single-lane road (4.0m lane width), outer lane width 2.5m, footway width 2.0m each side, roadway width 13.0m, road reserve width 20.0m. An escalation note is appropriate because a single-lane road in a town context may create access and safety issues if traffic volume is growing. Consider whether upgrade to Gravel B (2-lane) is warranted.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies Gravel C as single-lane (1 lane, 4.0m carriageway) even in town context
Uses town gravel cross-section (not rural) because the road is in a trading centre
Includes footway width (2.0m) in the town cross-section -- does not omit pedestrian provision
Raises escalation or advisory note about a single-lane road in a town context
Does not incorrectly apply rural cross-section parameters to the town section

evals.prompt: Determine the cross-section parameters for a Class A road in Kenya using design standard DR2. The road is an inter-urban highway on flat terrain. Using the Kenya Road Design Manual RDM 1.3, specify the lane width, shoulder width, carriageway configuration, crossfall, and identify the standard cross-section type. Also note the expressway (DR1) cross-section for comparison.
evals.expected_output: From Kenya RDM Table 4.1: Class A lane width = 3.65 m. From Table 4.2: Class A shoulder width = 2.7 m. DR2 maps to cross-section R3 or R4 (Table 2.7). R3: 2-carriageway road with 3.65m lanes, 2.70m outer shoulder, 1.00m inner shoulder, 2.00m central median. If using R4: single carriageway with 3.65m lane and 2.50m shoulders. Crossfall: 2.5% (paved). For DR1 expressway comparison: R1 has 4 carriageways, frontage road, full access control.

assertions.text: Lane width is 3.65 m for Class A per Kenya RDM Table 4.1 (not 3.5 m Uganda value)
Shoulder width is 2.7 m for Class A per Table 4.2
Cross-section type identified as R3 or R4 for DR2 standard
Crossfall is 2.5% for paved road per Section 4.3.4
Expressway DR1 cross-section (R1) described with divided carriageway, median, and full access control

evals.prompt: Design the cross-section for a DC5 paved trunk road in Ethiopia on rolling terrain. Using the ERA Geometric Design Manual 2013 Tables 2-1 and 2-2, specify the carriageway width, shoulder widths, normal crossfall, and side slope ratios. The road has an AADT of 500 vehicles per day.
evals.expected_output: Per ERA GDM 2013 Tables 2-1 and 2-2 for DC5 paved: carriageway width 7.0 m, shoulder width 1.5 m (rolling terrain). Normal crossfall 2.5% for paved DC5. Side slopes per ERA Table 6-1. Note DC5 carriageway is 7.0 m (same as DC6) but shoulders are narrower (1.5 m vs 1.5-3.0 m for DC6). ERA cross-sections differ from Uganda and Kenya in the dual paved/unpaved standards for DC2-DC4.

assertions.text: References ERA GDM 2013 Tables 2-1 and 2-2 for cross-section dimensions (not Uganda MoWT or Kenya RDM)
DC5 paved carriageway width is 7.0 m per ERA Table 2-1
Shoulder width for DC5 on rolling terrain is 1.5 m per ERA Table 2-2
Normal crossfall is 2.5% for paved DC5 roads per ERA Tables 2-6 to 2-17
